title: Model-invoke type
description: Configure type = MODEL_INVOKE for provider-neutral operation bindings and model operations through a MODEL_WORKER backend.
openingSummary: MODEL_INVOKE is the WorkstationType for provider-neutral operation and operationBindings. Set type to MODEL_INVOKE when a step must run through a MODEL_WORKER with those operation surfaces, and choose behavior independently for STANDARD, REPEATER, CRON, or POLLER scheduling. Keep promptFile, outcomeFormat, outputSchema, and stopWords on MODEL_WORKSTATION instead.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: Model-invoke is the Factory WorkstationType for provider-neutral model operation bindings in you-agent-factory. This page covers the type = MODEL_INVOKE discriminator, how MODEL_INVOKE pairs with a MODEL_WORKER backend and exclusive operation and operationBindings fields, the validated type overlay embed, a minimal valid configuration, an incompatible misuse that borrows model-workstation outcomeFormat, operational cautions, and compatible WorkstationKind behaviors. Use it when a workstation must bind provider-neutral model operations rather than author prompt files and parse outcomes, run an agent loop, or dispatch inference, script, or poller workers.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: The discriminator field is type with value MODEL_INVOKE. MODEL_INVOKE selects the runtime that dispatches a named MODEL_WORKER for provider-neutral operation work and selects the exclusive operation and operationBindings fields. Type is independent of behavior: MODEL_INVOKE pairs with every published WorkstationKind, including STANDARD and CRON. MODEL_INVOKE is not a scheduling behavior and is not MODEL_WORKSTATION — keep promptFile, outcomeFormat, outputSchema, and stopWords on the model-workstation type. Both share MODEL_WORKER companions, but their selected field sets stay separate.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Author a workstation with type MODEL_INVOKE and bind a MODEL_WORKER. Choose behavior for the scheduling axis you need — STANDARD for fire-once dispatch, REPEATER for change-triggered loops, CRON for schedules, or POLLER for long-lived poller ingress. Wire inputs plus outputs, onContinue, onRejection, and onFailure so model outcomes route correctly. Set operation for the provider-neutral operation name, and use operationBindings when this step needs named binding maps. Do not set promptFile, outcomeFormat, outputSchema, stopWords, openCodeAgent, classificationRoutes, or cron fields that belong to other WorkstationType or WorkstationKind families. Choose MODEL_WORKSTATION for prompt-file and outcome-parse surfaces, AGENT_RUN for agent loops, or INFERENCE_RUN for bounded inference harness dispatch.

variantFields.title: Variant Fields
variantFields.body: The embed below shows the live Workstation base definition with MODEL_INVOKE overlay applicability. MODEL_INVOKE selects the exclusive operation and operationBindings fields. Excluded fields belong to other WorkstationType families or to CRON scheduling and must not appear on this type. Open the full Factory schema reference for exhaustive field lookup rather than copying contract text into this page.

examples.title: Examples
examples.body: Use the minimal valid example when starting a MODEL_INVOKE with behavior STANDARD, an operation, and optional operationBindings. The misuse example borrows outcomeFormat from MODEL_WORKSTATION — that field is excluded from MODEL_INVOKE.

operationalCautions.title: Operational Cautions
operationalCautions.body: MODEL_INVOKE steps fail closed when the bound worker is missing or is not a MODEL_WORKER, when concurrency or resource limits block dispatch, when route fields leave an outcome unmapped, or when authors place model-workstation, agent-run, classifier, or cron exclusives on this type. Do not use outcomeFormat under MODEL_INVOKE — that field belongs on MODEL_WORKSTATION. Keep scheduling on behavior and runtime on type.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is the direct /docs/workstations/model-invoke surface for MODEL_INVOKE runtime. It is not a sync of packaged CLI docs, not the MODEL_WORKSTATION type guide, and not the STANDARD, REPEATER, CRON, or POLLER behavior contracts. Compatible WorkstationKind companions name the scheduling pairing for this type; they do not redefine MODEL_INVOKE fields. Worker backends stay on the workers family.

links.companionIntro: MODEL_INVOKE requires a MODEL_WORKER companion and is compatible with every published WorkstationKind. Bind the model worker, then choose scheduling on the behavior axis. MODEL_WORKSTATION also uses MODEL_WORKER but selects prompt/parse surfaces instead of operation fields.
links.misuseNote: outcomeFormat belongs on MODEL_WORKSTATION. Keep MODEL_INVOKE on identity, worker binding, inputs, outputs, routing fields, and operation / operationBindings only.
links.failureWorkerMissingCause: No worker bound, or bound worker is not a MODEL_WORKER — author and bind a model backend
links.failureWorkerMismatchCause: Bound worker is AGENT_WORKER, INFERENCE_WORKER, or another non-model type — switch to MODEL_WORKER or change workstation type
links.failureConcurrencyCause: Workstation or resource limits blocked model dispatch — raise limits or reduce parallel work
links.failureRoutingCause: Outcome has no authored route — wire outputs, onContinue, onRejection, or onFailure
links.failureOutcomeFormatMisuseCause: outcomeFormat declared under MODEL_INVOKE — move prompt/parse surfaces to type MODEL_WORKSTATION
